Reproduction of p8-024-safetensors-header-int64-oom
====================================================

Environment: darwin/arm64, Go 1.26.1, ollama HEAD 57653b8e

Test 1: Direct convertFromSafetensors call with MaxInt64 header
----------------------------------------------------------------
Command: go test -v -run TestSafetensorsHeaderMaxInt64 ./server/
Result: PANIC: runtime error: makeslice: cap out of range
    (recovered by test's defer)

Test 2: Handler-like goroutine reproduction
--------------------------------------------
Command: go test -v -run TestCreateHandlerSafetensorsMaxInt64Panic ./server/
Result: goroutine panic ESCAPES - test process exits with FAIL
    panic: runtime error: makeslice: cap out of range

    goroutine 39 [running]:
    github.com/ollama/ollama/convert.parseSafetensors(...)
      /convert/reader_safetensors.go:39 +0x170
    github.com/ollama/ollama/convert.parseTensors(...)
      /convert/reader.go:94 +0xfc
    github.com/ollama/ollama/convert.ConvertModel(...)
      /convert/convert.go:381 +0x178
    github.com/ollama/ollama/server.convertFromSafetensors(...)
      /server/create.go:441 +0x58c

Key observations
-----------------
- Binary int64 header of 0x7FFFFFFFFFFFFFFF (MaxInt64) -> make([]byte, 0, MaxInt64)
- On this platform, the runtime rejects allocations above ~1 PB with
  "makeslice: cap out of range" (recoverable panic), NOT unrecoverable OOM.
- However, the goroutine spawned in CreateHandler at server/create.go:99 has
  NO defer/recover(). gin.Recovery only guards the request handler goroutine,
  not this detached worker.
- Therefore the panic escapes and crashes the Ollama server process.

Claim in finding vs observed behavior
--------------------------------------
- Finding claim: "unrecoverable OOM via runtime.mallocgc -> mmap"
- Observed: panic in makeslice due to Go's maxAlloc check (2^40 on typical 64-bit)
  Both ultimately cause process termination since the goroutine is unrecovered,
  but the mechanism stated in the finding is incorrect in this platform/build.

On Linux/amd64 the behavior may be identical (makeslice cap-out-of-range) or
may actually reach mmap if maxAlloc allows the allocation; in either case
the process dies.

Entry point reachability
-------------------------
Attacker flow:
1. POST /api/blobs/:digest  (upload 8-byte safetensors blob)
2. POST /api/create with Files = {"x.safetensors": <digest>, "config.json": ..., "tokenizer.json": ...}
3. CreateHandler -> convertModelFromFiles -> convertFromSafetensors
   -> convert.ConvertModel -> parseTensors -> parseSafetensors
4. parseSafetensors reads int64 n = MaxInt64, make([]byte, 0, n) panics
5. Panic escapes unrecovered goroutine -> server process exit

Authentication: none. Exposure: 127.0.0.1 by default but operators commonly
set OLLAMA_HOST=0.0.0.0 for LAN access. allowedHostsMiddleware only blocks
non-loopback Host headers when the server is bound to loopback - does not
block authenticated attackers or LAN users.
